topOpportunities.title: Externalize internal workflow assets as paid automation packs
topOpportunities.thesis: The repo already contains reusable internal skills (browser-login-runbook, changelog-delta-summarizer, initiative-bootstrapper, landing-page-launcher). Package the most repeatable workflow into a sellable automation pack or implementation offer.
topOpportunities.monetizationRoute: Paid workflow packs plus setup assistance

demandSignals.description: Detected 8 internal skill assets suitable for external packaging.

topOpportunities.experimentPlan: Convert the highest-reuse internal skill into a documented, supportable automation pack and validate it on one marketplace plus one owned landing page.

topOpportunities.title: Operational audit packs for SMB service businesses
topOpportunities.thesis: Package agent-assisted audits, fix plans, and tracked automation setup into a repeatable paid offer with strong margin and fast proof-of-value.
topOpportunities.monetizationRoute: Fixed-fee audit plus recurring optimization retainer

demandSignals.description: Manual ops and quoting friction remain common in local businesses.
Businesses want AI help but need bounded and accountable delivery.

topOpportunities.experimentPlan: Launch one niche-specific landing page, outbound-compatible intake flow, and paid diagnostic offer capped at low acquisition spend.

sourceEvidence.note: Bootstrap fallback opportunity seeded from the repo lane catalog.

topOpportunities.title: asiifdev/business-leads-ai-automation operator-mapped opportunity
topOpportunities.thesis: GitHub demand signal from GitHub repositories: lead systems and SEO tooling: asiifdev/business-leads-ai-automation. ⚡ Open source lead generation & marketing automation. Google Maps scraper + AI-powered email/WhatsApp templates. Perfect for SMEs & agencies. Convert the repeated workflow into a bounded offer, hosted tool, or paid implementation pack.
topOpportunities.monetizationRoute: Lead fees, affiliate payouts, and productized implementation upsell

demandSignals.source: GitHub repositories: lead systems and SEO tooling
demandSignals.description: ⚡ Open source lead generation & marketing automation. Google Maps scraper + AI-powered email/WhatsApp templates. Perfect for SMEs & agencies. (61 GitHub stars).

topOpportunities.experimentPlan: Launch a narrow comparison or lead-capture surface around the strongest repeated pain cluster and route the first qualified demand.

sourceEvidence.note: GitHub repository signal from GitHub repositories: lead systems and SEO tooling: asiifdev/business-leads-ai-automation

topOpportunities.title: Automation packs sold through compliant marketplaces
topOpportunities.thesis: Externalize internal workflow templates and automation bundles into small-ticket digital products that can compound with support upsells.
topOpportunities.monetizationRoute: Marketplace sales plus support upsell and bundle expansion

demandSignals.description: Templates and automation kits remain attractive for buyers wanting fast implementation.
Marketplace demand can shorten time to first sale if listing quality is high.

topOpportunities.experimentPlan: Package two internal workflow assets into a starter bundle and A/B test listings across one primary marketplace and one owned landing page.

sourceEvidence.note: Bootstrap fallback opportunity seeded from the repo lane catalog.

A free social media automation platform that organically grows your online presence and gets you more followers. Convert the repeated workflow into a bounded offer, hosted tool, or paid implementation pack.
topOpportunities.monetizationRoute: Template bundle sales plus setup or support upsell

demandSignals.description: A free social media automation platform that organically grows your online presence and gets you more followers. (46 GitHub stars).

topOpportunities.experimentPlan: Package a narrow automation or template offer around the strongest repeated repo pattern and validate it on one owned page plus one marketplace.

activeExperiments.title: Experiment: Externalize internal workflow assets as paid automation packs
activeExperiments.thesis: Convert the highest-reuse internal skill into a documented, supportable automation pack and validate it on one marketplace plus one owned landing page.
successMetrics: Positive contribution margin within budget envelope
launchChecklist: Define asset package and offer page
Confirm treasury category and spend tags
Create kill and compound review dates
measurementPlan: Capture spend, attributable revenue, and response rate daily
Review conversion and payback time every 24 hours during launch week
Update portfolio score after each milestone or anomaly
activeExperiments.killThreshold: Kill if no meaningful traction signal appears before 60% of budget cap is consumed.
activeExperiments.compoundThreshold: Compound if initial payback is visible and distribution can be expanded without sharply raising CAC.

profiles.purpose: Default managed-browser lane for research and low-trust site work.
Attached local Chrome for company-authenticated sessions and operator-visible debugging.
Parallel Steel research sessions with isolated, low-trust state.
Steel namespace for new company signups and verification flows.
Steel namespace for the company Gmail inbox and email-confirmation flows.
Steel namespace for Wise browser fallback tasks when token APIs are insufficient.
Steel namespace for Hetzner console and infrastructure tasks.
Steel namespace for scalable marketplace listing operations.

reasons: OpenClaw managed browser is the default typed lane for public or low-trust work.
Treasury and infrastructure work will not silently degrade into the generic managed browser.
Attached Chrome is not fully ready through pairing, token auth, remote gateway reachability, and node host.
Steel is not configured for auth-sensitive browser work.
Company-auth work that needs persistent state will not silently fall back to the generic managed browser.

anchors.anchor: ChatGPT Pro and Business frame GPT access in ChatGPT as unlimited or effectively unlimited, subject to guardrails.
anchors.note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openai-pro-plan:ua-fetch-fallback, openai-business-limits:ua-fetch-fallback.

anchors.anchor: GPT-5.4 Pro remains the frontier OpenAI model page with a 1,050,000 token context window claim.
anchors.note: The latest GPT-5.4 Pro page loads, but a real rendered or browser-backed confirmation is still needed to restate the frontier or 1.05M wording precisely. Methods: openai-gpt-5.4-model:direct-fetch.

anchors.anchor: This repo should keep GPT-5.4 as the strategic frontier target while treating Codex-surface defaults as a separately verified runtime fact.
anchors.note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openai-gpt-5.4-model:direct-fetch, openai-codex-plan:ua-fetch-fallback, openai-codex-upgrades:ua-fetch-fallback, openai-codex-app:ua-fetch-fallback.

anchors.anchor: OpenClaw provider examples can lag the frontier model naming, so provider-model promotion must be runtime-probed.
anchors.note: The provider docs still need a clearer rendered read before restating the exact compatibility fallback assumptions automatically. Methods: openclaw-model-providers:direct-fetch, openai-gpt-5.4-model:direct-fetch.

anchors.anchor: Codex supports ChatGPT sign-in and plan-based access.
anchors.note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openai-codex-plan:ua-fetch-fallback, openai-codex-upgrades:ua-fetch-fallback.

anchors.anchor: Codex plan usage windows are separate from ChatGPT chat usage and require pacing discipline.
anchors.note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openai-codex-plan:ua-fetch-fallback.

anchors.anchor: OpenClaw officially supports Codex OAuth or ChatGPT sign-in for external workflows.
anchors.note: OpenClaw provider docs still explicitly document OpenAI Codex OAuth support. Methods: openclaw-model-providers:direct-fetch.

anchors.anchor: OpenClaw recommends Node for stable gateways and does not recommend Bun for gateway installs.
anchors.note: The current CLI docs could not be parsed cleanly enough to restate the Node-versus-Bun guidance automatically. Methods: openclaw-cli:direct-fetch.

anchors.anchor: The strongest always-on OpenClaw pattern is one primary loopback-bound gateway with secure remote access and remote nodes where needed.
anchors.note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openclaw-multiple-gateways:direct-fetch, openclaw-remote-access:search-backed, openclaw-nodes:search-backed.

anchors.anchor: Cron and heartbeat serve different purposes and should be combined with explicit wake or continuation flows.
anchors.note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openclaw-cron-vs-heartbeat:direct-fetch, openclaw-nodes:search-backed.

anchors.anchor: OpenClaw memory is Markdown-first, and Codex OAuth does not satisfy embeddings for memory search.
anchors.note: The latest memory docs still show Markdown-first memory and separate embedding requirements. Methods: openclaw-memory:direct-fetch.

anchors.anchor: OpenClaw supports QMD and local-first memory search backends.
anchors.note: The memory docs still advertise QMD and local-first search options. Methods: openclaw-memory:direct-fetch.

anchors.anchor: OpenClaw skills, plugins, and skill folders are supply-chain risk and must be treated as trusted or untrusted code accordingly.
anchors.note: OpenClaw security guidance still treats skills and plugins as trusted-code or supply-chain risk surfaces. Methods: openclaw-security:direct-fetch, openclaw-secrets:direct-fetch.

anchors.anchor: OpenClaw current docs expose strict config requirements, additive SecretRefs, and a first-class PDF tool.
anchors.note: Current OpenClaw docs still expose config requirements, broader secret coverage, and PDF tooling. Methods: openclaw-configuration:direct-fetch, openclaw-secrets:direct-fetch, openclaw-pdf:direct-fetch.

anchors.anchor: OpenClaw can use a managed browser, attached Chrome relay, and remote nodes; headless mode can trigger more anti-bot friction.
anchors.note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openclaw-browser:direct-fetch, openclaw-browser-login:direct-fetch, openclaw-nodes:search-backed, openclaw-faq:direct-fetch.

anchors.anchor: Automated self-update is possible but not the primary recommended operating pattern.
anchors.note: The latest FAQ needs a human confirmation of the self-update guidance. Methods: openclaw-faq:direct-fetch.

anchors.anchor: Wise personal tokens and OAuth 2.0 support different automation realities, and EU or UK PSD2 rules constrain some personal-token actions.
anchors.note: Current Wise docs still separate personal-token and OAuth realities and preserve PSD2 constraints. Methods: wise-security-access:direct-fetch, wise-personal-tokens:direct-fetch, wise-getting-started:direct-fetch.

anchors.anchor: Steel Cloud and Steel Local differ materially; auth-sensitive credential injection is not a safe assumption in Steel Local.
anchors.note: Current Steel docs still distinguish Cloud from Local and separate credential or profile capabilities accordingly. Methods: steel-sessions-overview:direct-fetch, steel-profiles:direct-fetch, steel-credentials:direct-fetch, steel-local-vs-cloud:direct-fetch.

notes: Use prod only after stage smoke, source refresh, treasury probe, and browser broker checks all pass.
Keep GPT-5.4 as the strategic model target, while separating official frontier-model truth, Codex-surface docs, and live OpenClaw provider proof.
The dispatcher should continue work immediately after task completion and keep multiple specialists occupied when ready work exists.
Treasury truth is unknown cash with unavailable ledger coverage.
blockers: Attached Chrome relay is not yet paired on the Windows browser host.
Steel is not fully configured for session automation yet.
One or more browser routes are intentionally blocked because no safe high-trust lane is ready.
